Off Calendar Jul-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Deborah Wilson, Esq. (510-465-7100) of McInerney & Dillon for Plaintiff.
Sean Ponist, Esq. (415-798-2222) of Ponist Law Goup for Defendant.

RULING - ARGUED. Plaintiff argues no positive misconduct of Counsel in totality.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with exception as follows:

DEFENDANTS TINA WU, DENISE LI, DAVIN CHAN, KING CHAN and CHENG LIU'S JOINT NOTICE OF Motion For Reconsideration Of Discovery Orders PURSUANT TO ccp 1008 OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR AN ORDER VACATING THESE ORDERS PURSUANT TO CCP 473(B). The tentative ruling is adopted in it's entirety except: Discovery responses may not assert any objections and sanctions to be paid by August 8, 2017.

As an invitation to the court to reconsider on its own motion the discovery orders per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1108. On its own motion and per Civil Code 473(b), subject to the conditions stated in this order, the court vacates all ten orders on the grounds that all those orders were the result of the positive misconduct of defendants' former counsel David Wiseblood in failing to provide any legal representation to the defendants with respect to the orders and the matters that led to those orders and defendants were not responsible for Mr. Wiseblood's misconduct nor did any conduct on their part lead to the entry of the orders. All ten orders are vacated subject to the following conditions: 1) the monetary sanctions set forth in those order remain in effect; 2) no later than August 8, 2017 defendants must provide verified code-compliant responses to all of the discovery requests that were at issue in all ten orders without asserting any objections of any kind except on grounds of privilege; 3) if defendants withhold any documents on grounds of privilege, no later than August 8, 2017 defendants must provide a privilege log as to all withheld documents; 4) if plaintiff Bay City View, LLC desires a continuance of the trial, defendants will stipulate to any trial continuance requested by Bay View that is for six months or less; and 5) if desired by Bay View, each of the defendants will make themselves available for deposition on a date requested by Bay View provided that Bay View give five calendar days' notice. These conditions substantially mitigate the harm to Bay View resulting from Mr. Wiseblood's positive misconduct that led to the entry of the ten orders. Plaintiff to submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)
